title: carex × pseudovulpina
intro: Carex × pseudovulpina, also known as False Fox Sedge, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, thanks to its saponins and essential oils. Carex × pseudovulpina can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it is also used in herbal remedies for its health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues and promoting skin health.
